content:
How to Apply to University of the Witwatersrand (Wits)

Thinking about studying at University of the Witwatersrand (Wits)? Based in Johannesburg, Gauteng, Wits is a Traditional University established in 1896. This guide covers everything you need to know to apply successfully — from APS requirements to application deadlines, bursaries, and step-by-step instructions.

At a Glance: Wits Fast Facts

DetailInformation
Full NameUniversity of the Witwatersrand (Wits)
TypeTraditional University
LocationBraamfontein, Johannesburg
Year Founded1896
Application Portalhttps://www.wits.ac.za/students/undergraduate/applying/
Application FeeR100
Applications OpenApril 1
Applications CloseSeptember 30
Minimum APS35 (varies by programme)
Contact Phone011 717 1000
Contact Emailapplications@wits.ac.za

Why Choose Wits?

Top 3 African universities; consistently in QS World Top 500.

  • Home to the Wits Cradle of Humankind Research Institute
  • One of the largest medical schools in sub-Saharan Africa
  • Wits Alumni include Nelson Mandela (enrolled but did not complete), Christiaan Barnard's colleague Hamilton Naki
  • Has the largest science park in Africa on its campus

Faculties and Programmes at Wits

Wits offers qualifications across 5 faculties:

  • Commerce, Law & Management
  • Engineering & the Built Environment
  • Health Sciences
  • Humanities
  • Science

Popular programmes include: Medicine (MBChB), Mining Engineering, Law (LLB), BCom, Architecture.

APS Requirements for Wits

The minimum Admission Point Score (APS) required at Wits is 35 points. However, individual programmes may require significantly higher scores:

APS range: 35–44 depending on faculty

Wits uses NBT results alongside NSC marks. Medicine requires 44+ APS. Engineering: 37+. Law: 38+. See the Wits APS calculator on their website.

How to Calculate Your APS

Your APS is calculated from your Grade 12 (NSC) subject results. Each subject receives a score from 1 to 7 based on your percentage:

Percentage (%)Rating CodeAPS Points
80–100%7 (Outstanding)7
70–79%6 (Meritorious)6
60–69%5 (Substantial)5
50–59%4 (Adequate)4
40–49%3 (Moderate)3
30–39%2 (Elementary)2
0–29%1 (Not Achieved)1

Your APS is the sum of your six best subjects (excluding Life Orientation, which is counted at half-value at most universities, or excluded entirely).

Use our free APS Calculator to calculate your score instantly.

Step-by-Step: How to Apply to Wits

Step 1: Check Programme Requirements

Before applying, confirm the specific APS requirements and subject prerequisites for your chosen programme at https://www.wits.ac.za. Some programmes require specific subjects (e.g., Mathematics for Engineering, Life Sciences for Medicine).

Step 2: Gather Your Documents

You will typically need:

  • Certified copy of your ID (South African ID or passport for international students)
  • Certified copy of your Grade 11 final results (if applying before matric)
  • Certified copy of your NSC / Grade 12 results (final matric results when available)
  • Proof of residence (e.g., utility bill)
  • Completed application form (online or paper)

Step 3: Apply Online

Visit the Wits online application portal at https://www.wits.ac.za/students/undergraduate/applying/. Create an account, complete the application form, upload your documents, and pay the application fee of R100.

Step 4: Submit Before the Deadline

Applications for 2027 admission typically open in April 1 and close on September 30. Apply as early as possible — popular programmes fill up quickly, especially Medicine, Law, and Engineering.

Step 5: Accept Your Offer

Once you receive a conditional or unconditional offer from Wits, accept it within the specified timeframe (usually 2–4 weeks). You may need to pay a registration deposit to secure your place.

Step 6: Register for Your Programme

After accepting your offer, complete the formal registration process in January (or mid-year for Semester 2 programmes). Bring original and certified copies of all documents.

Application Deadlines for Wits

Missing the application deadline is one of the most common reasons students don't get into their chosen institution. Here are the key dates:

  • Applications Open: April 1
  • Application Deadline: September 30
  • Results Notification: Typically November–December for the following year
  • Registration: January (Semester 1) or mid-year

Pro tip: Don't wait until the last day. Apply when applications open — some programmes are filled on a first-come, first-served basis.

Bursaries and Financial Aid at Wits

Studying at Wits can be financed through several bursaries and financial aid options:

  • NSFAS
  • Wits Financial Aid & Scholarships
  • Wits Vice-Chancellor's Scholarship
  • Oppenheimer Memorial Trust

NSFAS (National Student Financial Aid Scheme): If your combined household income is below R350,000 per year, you may qualify for NSFAS funding. Apply at www.nsfas.org.za from October each year for the following academic year.

Preparing for Wits While Still in School

The best time to prepare for university is while you're still in Grade 10, 11, or 12. Here's what you can do:

  • Focus on your key subjects: Make sure you're strong in the subjects required for your chosen programme
  • Use AI tutoring: StudyBuddy's AI tutor helps you master difficult CAPS topics before matric
  • Research your options early: Visit open days at Wits to understand campus life
  • Apply for bursaries early: Some bursaries (like Allan Gray, Anglo American) are available while you're still in Grade 11
  • Track your APS: Use our APS calculator to know where you stand

What Happens After You Apply?

After submitting your application to Wits, the process typically follows these stages:

  1. Application Confirmation: You'll receive an email or SMS confirming receipt of your application
  2. Conditional Offer: If your Grade 11 results meet the requirements, you may receive a conditional offer
  3. Final Results Submission: Submit your final NSC results in January
  4. Unconditional Offer: If your matric results meet or exceed the requirements, your offer becomes unconditional
  5. Registration: Register for your programme and pay your fees or confirm NSFAS/bursary funding

Comparing Wits With Other Universities

Not sure if Wits is the right fit? Here are some things to consider:

FactorWitsTypical Alternative
TypeTraditional UniversityTraditional University / UoT
LocationJohannesburgVaries
Minimum APS3520–37 (varies widely)
Application FeeR100R100–R400
NSFAS EligibleYesYes (public) / No (private)

Frequently Asked Questions About Applying to Wits

Still have questions? Our FAQ section below answers the most common questions about applying to Wits.

Need extra help with your Grade 12 subjects to meet Wits's entry requirements? StudyBuddy's AI tutor is FREE to start — get personalised help in Mathematics, Sciences, English, and all CAPS subjects. Most students improve their marks by 20–30% within a few weeks.
keywords: University of the Witwatersrand (Wits) how to apply
quickAnswer: To apply to University of the Witwatersrand (Wits), visit https://www.wits.ac.za/students/undergraduate/applying/, pay the R100 application fee, and submit your documents before September 30. Minimum APS is 35 for most programmes.

faqs.question: What is the minimum APS to apply to Wits?
faqs.answer: The minimum APS at Wits is 35 points for most programmes. However, competitive programmes like Medicine, Law, or Engineering may require significantly higher scores (35–44 depending on faculty). Always check the specific requirements for your chosen programme on the Wits website.

faqs.question: When do applications open for Wits?
faqs.answer: Applications for Wits typically open in April 1 and close on September 30. We strongly recommend applying as early as possible, as popular programmes fill up quickly. Visit https://www.wits.ac.za/students/undergraduate/applying/ to apply online.

faqs.question: How much is the application fee for Wits?
faqs.answer: The application fee at Wits is R100. Some students may qualify for a fee waiver — check the Wits website for details. The application fee is usually non-refundable even if your application is unsuccessful.

faqs.question: Does Wits accept NSFAS?
faqs.answer: Yes, Wits is an accredited NSFAS institution. If your household income is below R350,000 per year, you may qualify for NSFAS funding which can cover tuition, accommodation, meals, transport, and a living allowance. Apply at www.nsfas.org.za from October each year.

faqs.question: What documents do I need to apply to Wits?
faqs.answer: For your application to Wits, you will need: a certified copy of your ID, certified copies of your Grade 11 and/or Grade 12 results, proof of residence, and any programme-specific requirements (portfolio for arts, HPAT scores for health sciences, etc.). All documents should be certified within 3 months of the application date.
